text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
FIRST RESTAGING EXAMINATION — METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA
Yervoy / Opdivo COMBINATION IMMUNOTHERAPY, CYCLE 4 / WEEK 12 TIMEPOINT

---

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Okonkwo is a 63-year-old woman with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right-sided primary with prior sarcomatoid differentiation (~22%), pT3bN2 on final surgical pathology following cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection with IVC thrombectomy performed approximately 20 weeks prior. She was subsequently found to have bilateral pulmonary metastases on pre-therapy baseline CT. She has been receiving first-line systemic immunotherapy with ipi (1 mg/kg IV q3 weeks) and Opdivo (3 mg/kg IV q3 weeks) per the CheckMate 214 regimen. The third cycle of Yervoy was complicated by grade 2 immune-related colitis managed with budesonide, with ipi subsequently re-introduced and the fourth dose now completed. This examination is obtained for first formal restaging and response assessment, approximately 12 weeks after initiating combination immunotherapy.

PRIOR STUDIES FOR COMPARISON:
1. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast — pre-therapy baseline scan (approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy)
2. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast — initial staging scan at time of diagnosis

TECHNIQUE:
Helical multidetector CT was performed from the lung apices through the symphysis pubis following uneventful administration of 100 mL of intravenous low-osmolar iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350) in the portal venous phase, with additional delayed acquisitions through the upper abdomen for renal parenchymal and adrenal characterization. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and abdominal/pelvic soft tissue windows were reviewed. Reconstructions in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes were performed. There is no history of prior contrast reaction; creatinine reviewed and within acceptable threshold for contrast administration in this patient with a single functioning left kidney.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. There is no endobronchial lesion.

The previously identified bilateral pulmonary metastatic nodules have all decreased in size compared with the pre-therapy baseline examination:

— Right lower lobe: The dominant right lower lobe nodule, which measured 2.1 cm at the pre-therapy baseline, now measures approximately 1.3 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion retains smooth, well-defined margins without evidence of cavitation or internal necrosis. This represents a meaningful reduction in size.

— Right upper lobe: A right upper lobe nodule previously measuring 1.2 cm now measures approximately 0.7 cm. It remains solid in attenuation without significant internal change in character.

— Left lower lobe: A left lower lobe subpleural nodule previously measuring 0.9 cm has decreased to approximately 0.5 cm. It is still visible but considerably smaller and retains a rounded morphology.

— Left upper lobe: A small left upper lobe nodule previously measuring 0.7 cm now measures approximately 0.4 cm, approaching the lower limit of reliable measurement but representing a clear reduction from prior.

There are no new pulmonary nodules identified. No new ground-glass opacities, consolidations, or tree-in-bud opacities to suggest infectious or inflammatory superimposed process. No pneumothorax. No pleural effusions bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinal contours are within normal limits. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No anterior mediastinal mass. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal change. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for technique. No pericardial effusion. Central pulmonary vasculature unremarkable.

Chest Wall and Pleura:
No rib lesions, sternal lesions, or chest wall soft tissue masses. No pleural thickening or nodularity to suggest pleural involvement by metastatic disease.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with smooth contour and homogeneous parenchymal enhancement throughout. No focal hepatic lesions are identified on this examination. No biliary ductal dilation. The gallbladder is present without calculi or wall thickening.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size and enhancement pattern. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas:
The pancreas enhances homogeneously. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

Adrenal Glands:
The left adrenal gland is unremarkable in morphology and enhancement. The right adrenal gland appears normal without nodularity or enlargement on this examination. No discrete adrenal lesion identified at this time.

Kidneys and Retroperitoneum:
The patient is status post right radical nephrectomy. The right renal fossa demonstrates expected post-surgical changes: soft tissue stranding and fibrosis in the right retroperitoneum consistent with the prior operative bed, without discrete fluid collection, hematoma, or abscess formation. No residual or recurrent soft tissue mass is identified in the right renal fossa. The inferior vena cava is patent without evidence of residual or recurrent thrombus. The IVC is normal in caliber throughout its visualized length from the iliac confluence to the hepatic segment, including the infrarenal segment previously involved by tumor thrombus.

The left kidney is solitary and in its expected position. It demonstrates compensatory hypertrophy with normal contour and enhancement. No left renal mass or hydronephrosis. No perinephric stranding to suggest pyelonephritis or obstruction.

Retroperitoneal lymph nodes: No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the aortocaval, para-aortic, or right common iliac regions. The previously noted retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy — including the right para-aortic, aortocaval, and right common iliac chain nodes — is no longer identifiable as discrete lymph node masses. There is residual soft tissue in the aortocaval region consistent with post-surgical change and fibrosis after the prior retroperitoneal lymph node dissection.

Other Abdominal Structures:
The stomach, small bowel, and visualized large bowel are unremarkable without evidence of bowel wall thickening or obstruction. No free intraperitoneal fluid. No ascites. Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber.

---

PELVIS:

No pathologically enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes. The uterus and adnexa are not individually distinguishable given post-menopausal involution but are unremarkable. The urinary bladder is adequately distended with a smooth wall and no intraluminal filling defect. No pelvic free fluid.

---

BONE WINDOWS:

The visualized osseous structures of the chest, spine, pelvis, and proximal femora are intact without lytic or sclerotic lesions to raise concern for skeletal metastatic involvement. No pathologic fractures. Mild degenerative changes are noted at the lower thoracic and upper lumbar spine, age-appropriate in appearance.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. BILATERAL PULMONARY METASTASES — ALL DECREASED IN SIZE COMPARED WITH PRE-THERAPY BASELINE: The four previously documented bilateral pulmonary metastases have all undergone notable reduction in size following approximately 12 weeks of Yervoy/Opdivo immunotherapy. The dominant right lower lobe lesion has decreased from 2.1 cm to 1.3 cm. The right upper lobe lesion has decreased from 1.2 cm to 0.7 cm. The left lower lobe lesion has decreased from 0.9 cm to 0.5 cm, and the left upper lobe lesion from 0.7 cm to 0.4 cm. In aggregate, the combined burden of measurable pulmonary disease has diminished substantially, consistent with a meaningful objective response to systemic immunotherapy. All four lesions appear morphologically solid without cavitation.

2. NO NEW SITES OF METASTATIC DISEASE: No new pulmonary nodules, hepatic lesions, adrenal masses, retroperitoneal adenopathy, osseous lesions, or pelvic soft tissue deposits are identified.

3. RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND — UNREMARKABLE: The right adrenal gland does not demonstrate any nodularity or enlargement on this examination.

4. IVC AND RIGHT RENAL FOSSA — STATUS POST CYTOREDUCTIVE NEPHRECTOMY AND IVC THROMBECTOMY: Expected post-surgical changes in the right retroperitoneum. The IVC is patent without residual or recurrent thrombus. No local recurrence identified.

5. PREVIOUSLY DOCUMENTED RETROPERITONEAL LYMPHADENOPATHY — NOT IDENTIFIED AS DISCRETE ADENOPATHY: Residual post-surgical soft tissue changes in the retroperitoneum are present, but no discrete enlarged lymph nodes are seen in the prior aortocaval or para-aortic distribution.

6. SOLITARY LEFT KIDNEY — COMPENSATORY HYPERTROPHY WITHOUT HYDRONEPHROSIS OR PARENCHYMAL ABNORMALITY.

Overall, findings are consistent with a favorable response to combination checkpoint immunotherapy at this first restaging timepoint. Continued imaging follow-up is appropriate to monitor for durability of response or subsequent change.

Dictated by: Radiology Attending
Reviewed and electronically signed by: Radiology Attending, Diagnostic Radiology / Body Imaging
Ordering provider: Genitourinary Oncology Attending
